2026-04-06.build: Write 5 JS functions from scratch — no looking up
2026-04-06.tip: Start your blank-file habit today. Open a .ts file and write without pasting.

2026-04-07.build: Implement a sliding window max in TS from scratch
2026-04-07.problem: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock (LC #121)
2026-04-07.tip: Two pointer = two indices moving toward each other. Sliding window = two indices moving same direction.

2026-04-08.build: Rewrite yesterday's solution using ES6+ syntax only
2026-04-08.tip: ES6 destructuring + spread are used in almost every TS codebase. Get these cold.

2026-04-09.build: Build a frequency counter class in TS with typed generics
2026-04-09.tip: Most DSA problems solvable in O(n) use a hashmap. When stuck, ask: can a map help here?

2026-04-10.build: Define 5 interfaces and 3 type aliases for a mini e-commerce domain
2026-04-10.tip: In TS, prefer interfaces for objects you'll extend, type aliases for unions and primitives.

2026-04-11.build: Implement a Stack class in TS with full type safety
2026-04-11.tip: Stack = LIFO. Every 'matching pair' problem (brackets, tags, temperatures) uses a stack.

2026-04-12.build: Solve 3 problems from this week from memory — no hints
2026-04-12.problem: Review week: Contains Duplicate + Move Zeroes + Min Stack
2026-04-12.tip: Sunday = mistakes only. What did you get wrong this week? Fix those first.

2026-04-13.build: Implement a generic Queue<T> class in TypeScript
2026-04-13.tip: Monotonic stack pattern: maintain a stack where elements are always increasing or decreasing.

2026-04-14.build: Build an Animal class hierarchy in TS using inheritance and access modifiers
2026-04-14.tip: TS classes compile to JS prototypes. Understanding prototypes makes TS classes predictable.

2026-04-15.build: Implement a doubly linked list in TS from scratch
2026-04-15.tip: Draw the pointer manipulation before coding. Linked lists are 90% visualization.

2026-04-16.build: Write 5 custom utility types using Pick, Omit, Partial, Required, ReturnType
2026-04-16.tip: Fast/slow pointer: fast moves 2 steps, slow moves 1. They meet if there's a cycle.

2026-04-17.build: Implement map, filter, reduce from scratch in TS without using built-ins
2026-04-17.tip: Implementing higher-order functions from scratch is a common TS interview warm-up.

2026-04-18.build: Structure a small TS project with 3 modules importing from each other
2026-04-18.tip: Use two pointers: advance one by N first, then move both until the first hits null.

2026-04-19.build: Build a mini in-memory key-value store in TS — set, get, delete, TTL
2026-04-19.problem: Review: Reorder List + Sliding Window Maximum
2026-04-19.tip: Sunday review. Re-solve your two hardest problems from this week without hints.

2026-04-20.build: Implement binary search in TS — classic, leftmost, rightmost variants
2026-04-20.tip: Always use lo + Math.floor((hi-lo)/2) to avoid integer overflow. Make this a habit.

2026-04-21.build: Write a typed fetch wrapper in TS with error handling and generics
2026-04-21.tip: In rotated array, one half is always sorted. Check which half and binary search accordingly.

2026-04-22.build: Implement a BinaryTree class in TS with inorder, preorder, postorder methods
2026-04-22.tip: Tree recursion template: if(!node) return null; left = recurse(left); right = recurse(right); combine.

2026-04-23.build: Timed session: solve 2 problems in 45 minutes — one array, one linked list
2026-04-23.problem: Final April review: Level Order Traversal + Find Minimum in Rotated Array
2026-04-23.tip: April ends here. Checkpoint: can you write a 100-line TS program without referencing docs?

2026-05-01.build: Re-solve Two Sum and Valid Anagram in Python — feel the syntax difference
2026-05-01.tip: Python day 1. Focus only on syntax today: indentation, f-strings, range(), enumerate(), zip().

2026-05-02.build: Solve 3 problems using Counter and defaultdict only — feel their power
2026-05-02.tip: defaultdict(list) eliminates the 'if key not in dict' check. Use it everywhere in DSA.

2026-05-03.build: Rewrite 5 for-loop solutions using list/dict comprehensions
2026-05-03.tip: Comprehensions are not just syntax sugar — they signal Pythonic thinking to interviewers.

2026-05-04.build: Run: git log --oneline, git bisect, git blame on any open source repo
2026-05-04.problem: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters (Python, LC #3)
2026-05-04.tip: Git bisect finds the commit that introduced a bug using binary search on your commit history.

2026-05-05.build: Implement top-K frequent elements using heapq in Python
2026-05-05.problem: Kth Largest Element in Array (Python, LC #215)
2026-05-05.tip: Python's heapq is a min-heap by default. For max-heap, negate the values.

2026-05-06.build: Write 3 mapped types and 2 conditional types in TS
2026-05-06.tip: TS conditional types: T extends U ? X : Y. These show up in advanced codebases constantly.

2026-05-07.build: Build a small Python CLI task manager: add, list, complete, delete tasks
2026-05-07.problem: Review week: Top K Frequent Elements + Valid Parentheses (Python)
2026-05-07.tip: Sunday build day. This CLI project practices OOP, file I/O, and clean code in Python.

2026-05-08.build: Use bisect.insort to maintain a sorted list dynamically
2026-05-08.problem: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array (Python, LC #153)
2026-05-08.tip: bisect.bisect_left and bisect_right are Python's built-in binary search. Know both.

2026-05-09.build: Build a LinkedList class in Python with __iter__, __len__, __repr__
2026-05-09.tip: Dunder methods make your classes feel native to Python. __repr__ is the most useful one.

2026-05-10.build: Clone expressjs/express — navigate to Router class, understand the structure
2026-05-10.problem: Search in Rotated Sorted Array (Python, LC #33)
2026-05-10.tip: In VSCode: Cmd+click to follow definitions. F12 to go to definition. Shift+F12 for all usages.

2026-05-11.build: Implement BST insert, search, delete in Python from scratch
2026-05-11.tip: BST validation trick: pass min/max bounds down the recursion, not just left/right comparison.

2026-05-12.build: Set 3 breakpoints in a Node.js/TS project, inspect call stack and variables
2026-05-12.problem: Lowest Common Ancestor of BST (Python, LC #235)
2026-05-12.tip: The call stack panel in VSCode debugger is one of the most valuable tools for navigating unfamiliar code.

2026-05-13.build: Build a Python BankAccount class — deposit, withdraw, transaction history, overdraft protection
2026-05-13.problem: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum (Python, LC #124)
2026-05-13.tip: Sunday build: focus on clean code — no spaghetti, proper class separation, docstrings.

2026-05-14.build: Build an adjacency list graph class in Python
2026-05-14.tip: Number of Islands = gateway graph problem. Master DFS and BFS solutions for this one.

2026-05-15.build: Write a Python script demonstrating threading vs multiprocessing
2026-05-15.tip: GIL means Python threads don't run in parallel for CPU tasks. Use multiprocessing for that.

2026-05-16.build: Write a @log decorator in TS that logs method calls and execution time
2026-05-16.tip: Decorators in TS are just higher-order functions. NestJS uses them everywhere — understanding them unlocks reading NestJS codebases.

2026-05-17.build: Timed: 45 min mock session — 2 graph problems back to back
2026-05-17.problem: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow (LC #417) + Course Schedule II
2026-05-17.tip: During mocks, say what you're thinking. Interviewers give partial credit for the right approach.

2026-05-18.build: Implement Union-Find (DSU) with path compression in Python from scratch
2026-05-18.tip: Implement Union-Find 3 times this week until you can write it in under 5 minutes cold.

2026-05-19.build: Navigate prisma/prisma repo — find where query building happens
2026-05-19.tip: Reading real codebases: don't read linearly. Start from an entry point, follow the call chain.

2026-05-20.build: Koko Eating Bananas — implement in Python, then rewrite in TS
2026-05-20.tip: 'Binary search on answer' pattern: when asked to minimize/maximize a value, BS on the answer space.

2026-05-21.build: Sunday: full review + re-solve 5 problems you found hardest this month
2026-05-21.tip: May ends next week. Checkpoint: can you solve any easy in under 10 min in Python?

2026-05-22.build: Implement MinHeap class in Python from scratch — insert, extract_min, heapify
2026-05-22.tip: Building a heap from scratch is a common follow-up. Practice it until it takes under 10 min.

2026-05-23.build: Build a typed PriorityQueue<T> class in TypeScript
2026-05-23.problem: Find Median from Data Stream (Python, LC #295)
2026-05-23.tip: Two heaps pattern: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half. Median is always at the tops.

2026-05-24.build: Read 3 merged PRs on expressjs/express — understand what changed and why
2026-05-24.tip: PR reading skill: look at the issue it references first, then the diff. Understand the 'why' not just the 'what'.

2026-05-25.build: Build a TS REST API — 3 endpoints, typed req/res, error middleware
2026-05-25.tip: Dijkstra: use a min-heap. Pop the node with shortest distance, relax its neighbors.

2026-05-26.build: Timed mock: 45 min, 2 problems — 1 graph + 1 heap
2026-05-26.tip: Word Ladder is BFS with a clever trick: treat each word as a node, adjacent = 1 letter change.

2026-05-27.build: Re-implement binary search tree in TS with full typed generics
2026-05-27.tip: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order. The Kth element inorder = Kth smallest.

2026-05-28.build: Sunday: build a Python Splitwise-lite: add expense, settle up, show balances
2026-05-28.problem: Review: Merge K Sorted Lists + Find Median from Data Stream
2026-05-28.tip: Splitwise-lite is a great LLD practice piece — it uses hashmaps, user objects, and clean separation.

2026-05-29.build: Implement Fibonacci 3 ways: recursive, memoized, tabulated
2026-05-29.tip: Before any DP problem write: 'dp[i] means...' in a comment. Clarity on state = clarity on solution.

2026-05-30.build: Implement House Robber with both top-down memo and bottom-up tabulation
2026-05-30.tip: House Robber pattern: at each house, max(rob_this + dp[i-2], skip = dp[i-1]).

2026-05-31.build: May final review: write one function in Python and one in TS from scratch without any reference
2026-05-31.tip: May checkpoint: you should solve any easy in under 10 min Python and debug TS code comfortably.

2026-06-01.build: Fill a 2D DP table by hand for Unique Paths — then code it
2026-06-01.tip: For 2D DP: always draw the table and fill small examples by hand before coding.

2026-06-02.build: Find SOLID violations in a messy TS class you write — then refactor it
2026-06-02.tip: LCS table: dp[i][j] = length of LCS of first i chars of s1 and j chars of s2.

2026-06-03.build: Navigate a NestJS starter project — identify controllers, services, modules
2026-06-03.tip: Edit distance = insert, delete, replace. dp[i][j] = min operations to convert s1[:i] to s2[:j].

2026-06-04.build: Implement Factory and Strategy patterns in TS with a real example
2026-06-04.tip: Factory pattern = delegate object creation. Strategy pattern = swap algorithms at runtime.

2026-06-05.build: Design Parking Lot in TS: ParkingLot, Floor, Spot, Vehicle classes
2026-06-05.tip: Parking Lot: think about what changes (spot occupancy) vs what's constant (lot structure).

2026-06-06.build: Saturday mock: 60 min — 1 DP problem + 1 graph problem timed
2026-06-06.problem: House Robber II (LC #213) + Number of Islands
2026-06-06.tip: For House Robber II (circular): run House Robber twice — once skip first, once skip last.

2026-06-07.build: Sunday: extend Parking Lot design — add pricing, reservations, receipts
2026-06-07.problem: Review: Edit Distance + Partition Equal Subset Sum
2026-06-07.tip: Extending a design you built is the best LLD practice. 'What if we add X?' is the real interview.

2026-06-08.build: Implement permutations generator in Python using backtracking
2026-06-08.tip: Backtracking template: choose → recurse → unchoose. The 'unchoose' step is what most people forget.

2026-06-09.build: Build Observer pattern in TS — EventEmitter from scratch, typed events
2026-06-09.tip: Subsets II: sort first, then skip duplicates at the same recursion level.

2026-06-10.build: Implement Trie in Python with insert, search, startsWith methods
2026-06-10.tip: Trie node = dict of children + is_end flag. Each character is a key in the dict.

2026-06-11.build: Read machine coding checklist: requirements → design → implement → test
2026-06-11.tip: Machine coding: spend 10 min on design before writing any code. Interviewers notice.

2026-06-12.build: Machine coding: build Snake game in TS — 90 min timed
2026-06-12.problem: Word Search II (Python, LC #212) — Trie + backtracking
2026-06-12.tip: Snake game: Board, Snake, Food, Direction classes. Game loop with collision detection.

2026-06-13.build: Debug exercise: introduce 3 bugs in your Parking Lot code and fix using VSCode debugger
2026-06-13.tip: LIS: dp[i] = length of longest increasing subsequence ending at index i. Answer = max(dp).

2026-06-14.build: Saturday mock: 90 min machine coding — build a task manager CLI in TS
2026-06-14.problem: Design Add and Search Words (Python, LC #211)
2026-06-14.tip: Task manager: Task, TaskList, Priority classes. JSON persistence. Clean CLI interface.

2026-06-15.build: Sunday: full review — list every pattern you know, rate confidence 1–5
2026-06-15.problem: Re-solve your 3 hardest problems from June so far
2026-06-15.tip: Self-assessment is a skill. Knowing your weak patterns = knowing what to study next.

2026-06-16.build: Implement Decorator pattern in TS — add logging and caching to a service class
2026-06-16.tip: Decorator pattern ≠ TS decorators syntax. Understand the OOP pattern first.

2026-06-17.build: Write SQL queries: JOIN, GROUP BY, subqueries, window functions
2026-06-17.tip: Jump Game II: greedy — track the furthest you can reach, expand when you hit the current boundary.

2026-06-18.build: Read Nest.js source — find where @Injectable decorator is implemented
2026-06-18.tip: Gas Station: if total gas >= total cost, a solution exists. Start from the point where running sum < 0.

2026-06-19.build: Review your Snake game — refactor using Strategy pattern for movement
2026-06-19.tip: LRU Cache = hashmap + doubly linked list. Hashmap for O(1) access, DLL for O(1) reorder.

2026-06-20.build: Implement Dijkstra from scratch in Python — no library, just heapq
2026-06-20.problem: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops (Python, LC #787)
2026-06-20.tip: Modified Dijkstra: carry number of stops as part of the state (dist, stops, node).

2026-06-21.build: Saturday mock: 45 min DSA — 1 DP hard + 1 graph medium
2026-06-21.problem: Burst Balloons (Python, LC #312) — study the approach
2026-06-21.tip: Interval DP: think about what to do last, not first. Burst Balloons: which balloon to burst last?

2026-06-22.build: Sunday: Design URL Shortener on paper — classes, interfaces, storage layer
2026-06-22.problem: Review: LRU Cache + Word Search II
2026-06-22.tip: LLD on paper first, code second. Draw class diagram → identify relationships → then implement.

2026-06-23.build: Implement URL Shortener in TS: UrlShortener, UrlRepository, HashGenerator classes
2026-06-23.tip: URL Shortener: think about collision handling, custom aliases, and expiry from the start.

2026-06-24.build: Read 5 PRs on a TS repo — summarize what each one was trying to achieve
2026-06-24.tip: Word Break: dp[i] = True if s[:i] can be segmented. For each i, check all j < i.

2026-06-25.build: Extend URL Shortener — add rate limiting and analytics tracking
2026-06-25.tip: Extend your designs every time. 'What if we add analytics?' is a standard interview follow-up.

2026-06-26.build: Timed mock: 60 min — 1 LLD design question (explain out loud)
2026-06-26.tip: Meeting Rooms II: sort by start, use a min-heap of end times. Heap size = rooms needed.

2026-06-27.build: Saturday: build a small TS project with Docker — Dockerfile + docker-compose
2026-06-27.tip: Non-overlapping: sort by end time. Greedy: always keep the interval that ends earliest.

2026-06-28.build: Sunday: June retrospective. List all LLD patterns you know. Rate each 1–5.
2026-06-28.tip: June checkpoint: can you design a system from scratch and talk through it for 20 minutes?

2026-06-29.build: Debug an unfamiliar Node.js codebase — introduce a bug, find it with debugger only
2026-06-29.tip: Reverse BFS trick: instead of flowing water from land, flow from oceans inward.

2026-06-30.build: June final: timed 45 min mock — 2 medium problems you haven't seen before
2026-06-30.tip: June ends. You should now feel comfortable with DP patterns and basic LLD design.

2026-07-01.build: Design Splitwise in Python: User, Group, Expense, Balance classes
2026-07-01.tip: Splitwise: focus on the simplify debts algorithm — reduce N^2 transactions to minimum.

2026-07-02.build: Draw a load balancer architecture diagram — round robin, least connections, IP hash
2026-07-02.tip: HLD for SDE 1: you need the concepts, not deep implementation. Explain trade-offs clearly.

2026-07-03.build: Design a caching layer: write-through vs write-back vs write-around trade-offs
2026-07-03.problem: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree (Python, LC #297)
2026-07-03.tip: Cache invalidation is the hardest part. Know: TTL, LRU eviction, cache stampede.

2026-07-04.build: Machine coding: Task Manager in TS — 90 min timed, clean OOP
2026-07-04.problem: Maximum Width of Binary Tree (Python, LC #662)
2026-07-04.tip: Task Manager: focus on extensibility. Can you add priorities? Due dates? Tags? Show that thinking.

2026-07-05.build: Saturday mock: 45 min DSA + 20 min HLD discussion out loud
2026-07-05.problem: Minimum Cost to Connect All Points (Python, LC #1584)
2026-07-05.tip: Prim's algorithm: greedy MST. Use a heap, always pick the cheapest edge to an unvisited node.

2026-07-06.build: Sunday: extend Splitwise — add currency conversion, expense categories
2026-07-06.problem: Review: Accounts Merge + Serialize Binary Tree
2026-07-06.tip: Extending your own designs is the best LLD prep. Think about what breaks when you add features.

2026-07-07.build: Write a comparison: when to use Postgres vs MongoDB vs Redis — with real examples
2026-07-07.tip: SQL vs NoSQL: it's about access patterns, not just scale. What queries will you run most?

2026-07-08.build: Design Chess in TS — Board, Piece, Move, King, Queen, rules engine
2026-07-08.tip: Chess LLD: model pieces polymorphically. Each piece class overrides isValidMove(). Board is passive.

2026-07-09.build: Implement 3 rate limiting algorithms in TS: token bucket, sliding window, fixed window
2026-07-09.tip: Token bucket = smooth traffic. Sliding window = precise limits. Fixed window = simplest, has edge case at boundary.

2026-07-10.build: Timed mock: 60 min machine coding — rate limiter with tests in TS
2026-07-10.tip: Time-based store: hashmap of key → sorted list of (timestamp, value) pairs. Binary search for query.

2026-07-11.build: Extend chess — add castling, en passant, check detection
2026-07-11.tip: N-Queens = backtracking with pruning. Track rows, cols, and both diagonals as sets.

2026-07-12.build: Saturday mock: 30 min HLD — design a URL shortener at scale, explain out loud
2026-07-12.tip: HLD mock: structure it — requirements → capacity estimate → components → data model → trade-offs.

2026-07-13.build: Sunday: LLD review — whiteboard all 4 designs from memory in 10 min each
2026-07-13.problem: Review: 3 hardest problems from July so far
2026-07-13.tip: Whiteboarding from memory reveals gaps. Note every place you hesitated — those are your weak spots.

2026-07-14.build: Implement LRU Cache in both Python and TS — 20 min each
2026-07-14.problem: LRU Cache (LC #146) — solve without any hints in under 20 min
2026-07-14.tip: LRU Cache benchmark: you should implement this cleanly in 20 min. It's asked by every company.

2026-07-15.build: Write a Dockerfile for your TS REST API. Build it, run it, curl it.
2026-07-15.problem: Design a Stack With Increment Operation (Python, LC #1381)
2026-07-15.tip: Dockerfile: FROM → WORKDIR → COPY package*.json → RUN npm install → COPY . → CMD.

2026-07-16.build: Docker compose: TS API + Postgres + Redis in one compose file
2026-07-16.tip: Insert/Delete/GetRandom: combine hashmap (for O(1) insert/delete) with array (for O(1) random).

2026-07-17.build: Timed mock: 45 min — 2 DP problems back to back
2026-07-17.tip: Regex matching DP: dp[i][j] = can pattern[:j] match text[:i]. Handle '.' and '*' separately.

2026-07-18.build: Saturday: full 90-min machine coding mock — build Splitwise CLI in TS
2026-07-18.tip: Tic-Tac-Toe: don't use O(n²) check. Track row, col, diagonal sums per player.

2026-07-19.build: Sunday: write out your 'design philosophy' — how you approach a system design question
2026-07-19.tip: Having a consistent approach to system design questions is as important as the content. Practice the structure.

2026-07-20.build: Speed drill: solve 5 easy problems in under 8 minutes each in Python
2026-07-20.tip: Histogram: monotonic stack, track indices. Pop when smaller bar found, calculate area.

2026-07-21.build: Navigate a new open-source TS project you've never seen — find a bug or improvement
2026-07-21.tip: Rain water: two-pointer. Track maxLeft and maxRight. Water at i = min(maxL, maxR) - height[i].

2026-07-22.build: Timed: 45 min mock DSA — 1 graph hard + 1 DP medium
2026-07-22.problem: Critical Connections in a Network (Python, LC #1192)
2026-07-22.tip: Bridges in graph: Tarjan's algorithm. Low-link values identify bridges.

2026-07-23.build: Thursday: 90 min timed — new machine coding problem you haven't seen
2026-07-23.tip: File system LLD: Directory node with children map. File node with content string. ls, mkdir, addFile, readFile.

2026-07-24.build: HLD mock: 30 min — design Instagram feed out loud. Record yourself.
2026-07-24.problem: Design Search Autocomplete System (LC #642) — study the approach
2026-07-24.tip: Recording yourself during HLD mocks is uncomfortable but reveals filler words, hesitations, gaps.

2026-07-25.build: Saturday: full mock — 45 min DSA + 30 min LLD + 20 min HLD back to back
2026-07-25.problem: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown (Python, LC #309)
2026-07-25.tip: Full mock day: simulate the real interview sequence. No breaks between sections.

2026-07-26.build: Sunday: July retrospective — list every system you can design. Rate each 1–5.
2026-07-26.tip: July checkpoint: can you implement LRU Cache in 20 min? Design parking lot in 15? If not, July isn't done.

2026-07-27.build: Draw Kafka architecture: producers, topics, partitions, consumers, offsets
2026-07-27.problem: Find All Anagrams in a String (Python, LC #438)
2026-07-27.tip: Message queues decouple producers from consumers. Know: ordering, at-least-once, exactly-once.

2026-07-28.build: Design a notification system using message queues — write the architecture
2026-07-28.problem: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons (Python, LC #452)
2026-07-28.tip: Interval greedy: sort by end. One arrow can burst all overlapping balloons.

2026-07-29.build: Timed mock: 45 min DSA — problems you've never seen before (random LC mediums)
2026-07-29.tip: Interval DP again. dp[i][j] = min turns to print s[i..j]. Think about where the first print ends.

2026-07-30.build: 90 min machine coding: build a logger system in TS — levels, transports, formatters
2026-07-30.tip: Logger: Strategy pattern for transports (console, file, remote). Chain of responsibility for log levels.

2026-07-31.build: July final: design Twitter feed system — write the full design doc
2026-07-31.tip: July done. August = mock mode. You should be able to solve mediums in under 25 min consistently.

2026-08-01.build: Full HLD mock: Twitter feed — 45 min, explain every decision
2026-08-01.tip: August = mock month. Every session should feel like an interview. No comfortable practice.

2026-08-02.build: Speed drill: 5 DP mediums, 25 min each, no hints
2026-08-02.problem: Burst Balloons + Strange Printer + Regular Expression Matching
2026-08-02.tip: If you're over 25 min on any medium, that's your weak spot. Drill that pattern specifically.

2026-08-03.build: Full HLD mock: WhatsApp — 45 min. Focus on message delivery guarantees.
2026-08-03.tip: WhatsApp HLD: online presence, message ordering, delivery receipts, group messages — each is a separate problem.

2026-08-04.build: 90 min machine coding mock: build an in-memory cache in TS with TTL and LRU eviction
2026-08-04.tip: Combining TTL + LRU: use a heap for TTL expiry, DLL for LRU order. Handle expiry lazily.

2026-08-05.build: Blind 75 audit: go through the list, mark every problem solved/unsolved/shaky
2026-08-05.problem: Median of Two Sorted Arrays (Python, LC #4)
2026-08-05.tip: If you have more than 10 'shaky' mediums on Blind 75, spend today re-solving the shakiest ones.

2026-08-06.build: LLD mock: design a food delivery system — 45 min, explain aloud
2026-08-06.tip: Food delivery LLD: Order, Restaurant, DeliveryAgent, Route, Payment. Focus on state transitions.

2026-08-07.build: Full simulation: 45 min DSA + 30 min LLD + 20 min HLD — back to back, no breaks
2026-08-07.tip: This is the hardest session. Your brain will tire after 45 min of DSA. Push through — that's the point.

2026-08-08.build: Saturday: record a mock HLD session — play it back and critique it
2026-08-08.tip: Dropbox HLD: chunked upload, dedup by hash, sync protocol, conflict resolution.

2026-08-09.build: Sunday: identify your 3 strongest patterns — prepare to lead with these in interviews
2026-08-09.tip: Know your strengths. Interviewers often let you pick the approach — lead with what you're confident in.

2026-08-10.build: Speed drill: 6 easy problems — under 8 min each. No exceptions.
2026-08-10.problem: Rotate Array + Valid Palindrome + Balanced Parentheses + Reverse LL + Max Depth Tree + Invert Tree
2026-08-10.tip: Eights are the benchmark: under 8 min for easy = you're interview-ready on fundamentals.

2026-08-11.build: Mock: 45 min DSA — 2 mediums you haven't seen (pick random LC mediums)
2026-08-11.tip: K closest: binary search for the left bound of the window. Window slides based on distance.

2026-08-12.build: Machine coding: build a mini pub/sub event system in TS — 90 min
2026-08-12.tip: Pub/sub: EventBus with subscribe(topic, handler) and publish(topic, data). Handle async delivery.

2026-08-13.build: HLD mock: design YouTube — 45 min. Focus on video storage and streaming.
2026-08-13.tip: YouTube HLD: video chunking, CDN for delivery, separate read/write paths, recommendation is a separate system.

2026-08-14.build: Full mock: 45 DSA + 30 LLD + 20 HLD — different topics from Aug 7
2026-08-14.tip: Compare this mock to Aug 7. Are you faster? Less stuck? Progress should be visible.

2026-08-15.build: Saturday: company-tagged problems — solve 3 Amazon-tagged and 3 Google-tagged mediums
2026-08-15.tip: Company tags on LC aren't guaranteed but give signal on what patterns each company favors.

2026-08-16.build: Sunday: the 'explain to a rubber duck' session — talk through 2 LLD designs out loud for 10 min each
2026-08-16.problem: Review: hardest 3 problems from August so far
2026-08-16.tip: If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough. The rubber duck exposes gaps.

2026-08-17.build: Hard problem session: 3 hard LC problems, no time limit, understand fully
2026-08-17.problem: Minimum Window Substring + Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum + Word Ladder II
2026-08-17.tip: Hard problems aren't about speed — they're about depth of understanding patterns.

2026-08-18.build: Machine coding: build a booking system in TS — seats, reservations, cancellations
2026-08-18.tip: Booking system: concurrency is the key challenge. How do you prevent double-booking?

2026-08-19.build: Speed audit: solve Blind 75 easy section entirely — 8 min per problem
2026-08-19.tip: If you're clean on all easies in under 8 min, your foundation is solid.

2026-08-20.build: LLD mock: design a notification system — 45 min, whiteboard + explain
2026-08-20.tip: Notification: multiple channels (push, SMS, email), user preferences, retry on failure, rate limiting per user.

2026-08-21.build: Full mock #3: 45 DSA + 30 LLD + 20 HLD — hardest versions of each
2026-08-21.tip: Three mocks in: you should feel the difference from mock 1. If not, revisit fundamentals.

2026-08-22.build: Saturday: containerize your best TS project — add GitHub Actions CI
2026-08-22.problem: Free choice: any problem you enjoy
2026-08-22.tip: Your GitHub should show active, clean projects. Interviewers do look at profiles.

2026-08-23.build: Sunday: final self-assessment — rate yourself 0–100 on every skill area
2026-08-23.problem: Review: your personal top 5 hardest problems ever
2026-08-23.tip: Be honest in your self-assessment. The goal is to know exactly what you know and don't.

2026-08-24.build: Final speed drills: graphs — 3 mediums under 20 min each
2026-08-24.problem: Swim in Rising Water (LC #778) + Reconstruct Itinerary (LC #332)
2026-08-24.tip: These are hard graph problems. If you can solve them, you're prepared for anything.

2026-08-25.build: Final machine coding: 90 min — most complex thing you can build
2026-08-25.problem: Design and implement a mini Redis (get, set, expire, pub/sub)
2026-08-25.tip: Mini Redis: this is a capstone machine coding challenge. Combine everything you know.

2026-08-26.build: Final HLD mock: design a ride-sharing system — 45 min
2026-08-26.tip: Uber HLD: location tracking (geohashing), matching algorithm, surge pricing, trip state machine.

2026-08-27.build: Final DSA mock: 45 min — 2 problems you've never seen before
2026-08-27.tip: Unknown problems are the real test. Trust your pattern recognition — you've built it over 5 months.

2026-08-28.build: Final LLD mock: design an e-commerce checkout system — 45 min
2026-08-28.problem: LLD: Design checkout (cart, payment, inventory, order)
2026-08-28.tip: Checkout: Cart, Order, Payment, Inventory, Notification — each is a bounded context. Keep them decoupled.

2026-08-29.build: Saturday: complete Blind 75 audit — every problem should be green
2026-08-29.tip: Blind 75 complete = interview ready. Any remaining gaps: fix today, not tomorrow.

2026-08-30.build: Full final simulation: 2 hour complete interview — DSA + LLD + HLD + behavioural
2026-08-30.tip: You started at 0% DSA in April. You're here now. The gap between you and an offer is just reps.

2026-08-31.build: Rest. Review your journey. Write down what you know now vs April 6.
2026-08-31.tip: 6 months done. You built this from zero. Go get the offer.
